summary: Jeff Atwood responds to Don Norman's criticism of Google's 'faux simplicity' by reframing the Google search box as the ultimate command prompt rather than a limited interface. He cites Bill de hÓra's observation that search may be replacing the desktop metaphor as the dominant computing paradigm after three decades. While acknowledging his unease about returning to command-line-style interfaces, Atwood concedes that typing to find what you want is often faster than visual navigation. The post questions whether usability experts need to reconsider their assumptions about interface design.
key_points: Don Norman's criticism that Google 'only does one thing' misses the point—the search box is a command prompt that can lead anywhere
The desktop metaphor, dominant for 30 years, may be giving way to a search-based paradigm
Google's oneboxes and integrated services demonstrate the search interface's hidden depth
Literal interpretations of the desktop metaphor (like Magic Cap) reveal its limitations
Typing and searching is often faster than point-and-click visual navigation, even within a single page
money_quotes: Once you realize that the Google search box is really a type of command prompt, the criticism that Google 'only does one thing' is... well, downright hilarious.
Search is the beginning of every command. You can go anywhere and do anything from there.

Perhaps the hunt and peck approach of searching (along with gaming) is becoming the dominant computing metaphor, replacing nearly 3 decades of user interfaces based on the metaphor of an office desktop.
There's a lot to be said for typing stuff to quickly get to what you want.

As much as I dislike the limitations of the desktop metaphor, I'm a little uneasy about the idea that the ultimate user interface we end up with in the next ten years will bear more resemblance to a command prompt.
I often bail out when attempting to visually point and click my way around crowded web pages; instead, I search for a relevant word on the page using incremental search.
key_insight: The Google search box isn't a dumbed-down interface—it's a command prompt that can lead anywhere, suggesting that search may be the new dominant computing metaphor.
